nodes.title: Samplers
widgets_values:
You can set for every video part a separate positive prompt on the samplers.
⬇️

nodes.title: Positive prompts
widgets_values:
Set your positive prompts for every stage here
➡️

nodes.title: Intermediate Videos
widgets_values:
This are the videos for every stage for reference and as OOM backup.
➡️

nodes.title: Basic usage
widgets_values:
## Usage

| Usage | (Number key) Shortcuts |
|---|---|
| 1. Set Checkpoints, Clip + VAE | (3) Checkpoint (subgraph) |
| 2. (optional) add LoRAs | (1) Settings, Prompt, Image, Video |
| 3. Enable Featuras you want with the "Features - Switch" | (1) Settings, Prompt, Image, Video |
| 4. Tune any parameter to your liking under "BASIC SETTINGS" | |
| 5. Add image you need | |
| 6. Write your prompts | (4) Samplers |
| 7. Enable as many samplers as you want the video going | |
| 8. Generate | |
|Optional: Tune any internal setting | (2) Backend (subgraph) |
🎦 Here will be your final video result.
🎦 Here will be your intermediate video result before upscaling or any other output feature.

nodes.title: Low LoRA
widgets_values: Set your low LoRAs for every stage here ➡️
✅ Here you set your initial image (frame) of your video.

nodes.title: Resolution
widgets_values:
⬆️ this will set the megapixel resolution of your video.
The logic scales the initial image to that MP and renders the video with that resolution.

nodes.title: BASIC settings
widgets_values:
| Setting | Default / Example | Description | |--------:|------------------:|------------:| | **CRF** | 20, webm, VP9 | Video compression level. Visually lossless. Set on VHS Node.| | **FPS** | 24 | Frames per second for videos. SVI works well with 24. | | **Shift** | 8 | Default 8. Adjust and test; WAN 2.2 SVI valid in range 8-12. | | **Steps total** | 6 | Total steps - node handles step math. More steps → higher quality but longer generation. Use even numbers. | | **Step Refiner** | 3 | Default 2 (50% of 4). Change to test different swap behavior. | | **Sampler** | euler | "Euler" works everytime. Other samplers can enhance detail or motion. This has major impact on how the video is generated. | | **Scheduler** | simple | "Simple" works eveytime. Other like "beta" and others can enhance some samplers. | | **Seed** | number + fixed | Every stage has a fixed seed. Set a specific seed to reproduce the same outcome. | | **Sage Attention** | disabled | Can be enabled **if installed correctly** to speed-up video generation times. | | **Watermark upload** | none | Here you can upload a watermark image and calibrate the settings to your liking (subgraph). | | **Positive prompt** | none | Write your positive prompt!
1) Brief description of the setting/appearance
2) Actions, motions, story
3) Camera composition
CFG 1 normally disables/ignores the negative prompt! NAG effectively enables negative prompting on CFG 1.| | **Negative prompt** | none | Write what you would like to exclude. Works only if CFG > 1 or NAG is enabled | | **Checkpoints** | Safetensors/GGUf |Choose the type of Checkpoint to use. Wan 2.2 uses a different diffusion model for the first steps (high noise) vs the last steps (low noise). You can ether use safetensors or gguf.| | **CLIP** | standard encoder | Use your preferred standard CLIP text encoder. No CLIP Vision H — not trained/implemented in WAN 2.2 and weights are disabled. | | **VAE** | same as WAN 2.1 | Still using the same VAE as WAN 2.1. |

nodes.title: Post-processing
widgets_values:
| Feature | Default / Example | Description |
|--------:|------------------:|------------:|
| Color match | off | This will try to match the color from the initial image to the samples. The color match algorithms default is set by default to '0.75'. Feel free to test other strength. |
| Interpolation | off | This will interpolate your video to double fps at the end. Interpolated FPS = 2 × FPS. |
| Upscale 2x (fast) | off | Final resolution = 2 × start resolution while maintaining aspect ratio. No morph; color-correct fast lanczos upscale. Low VRAM consumption. |
| Upscale /w Model (slow) | off | Final resolution depends on the used upscaler model; Can change/enhance details and high VRAM consumption. |
| RTX Super Resolution | off | 2x ultra fast accurate upscaling. RTX cards only. |
| Watermark | off | Enable for a watermark overlay on video. |

nodes.title: Video codec, CRF, compression and quality
widgets_values:
| Codec | CRF | Visual impact | Compatibility | Quality and compression |
|----------|-----|-------|-----|-----|
| AV1 | 30 | lossless | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| h265 | 19 | lossless | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| VP9 | 20 | lossless | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| h264 | 19 | lossless | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐ |


💡 *Tip* For AI-generated videos:
* AV1 (webm) or VP9 (webm) for websites
* AV1 (webm) or h265 (mp4) for local store

## Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │      └── umt5_xxl_fp8_e4m3fn_scaled.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 unet/
│   │      └── *.safetensors / *.gguf
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │      └── wan_2.1_vae.safetensors
```

widgets_values: ⬆️ Activate as many samplers as you like. Every sampler will expand the video with one full frame-count.

nodes.title: High LoRA
widgets_values: Set your high LoRAs for every stage here ➡️

nodes.title: Changelog
widgets_values:
## Requirements - [ComfyUI-VideoHelperSuite](https://github.com/Kosinkadink/ComfyUI-VideoHelperSuite) - [rgthree-comfy](https://github.com/rgthree/rgthree-comfy) - [Comfyui-WhiteRabbit](https://github.com/Artificial-Sweetener/comfyui-WhiteRabbit)
+ *packaging, torchlanc* - [ComfyUI-KJNodes](https://github.com/kijai/ComfyUI-KJNodes) - [ComfyUI-Frame-Interpolation](https://github.com/Fannovel16/ComfyUI-Frame-Interpolation) - [ComfyUI-GGUF](https://github.com/city96/ComfyUI-GGUF) - [ComfyUI-DaSiWa-Nodes](https://github.com/darksidewalker/ComfyUI-DaSiWa-Nodes) - [ffmpeg](https://www.ffmpeg.org/) - [Sage Attention](https://github.com/thu-ml/SageAttention) (optional) - [Upscaler model](https://openmodeldb.info/) (optional) --- ## Features - 🖼️ **SVI** (SVI 2.0 up to 10 samplers) - 🧩 **Automatic aspect‑ratio calculation and fitting** - ✨ **Ultra‑fast, color‑correct upscaling** (torchlanc) OR **Upscale with Model** - 🤝 **Video resolution matching** - Fully automatic scaling and resolution calculations - 🕠 **Length automation** - Fully automatic calculation of frame count - 🫥 **Watermark** support - 🪄 **Interpolation** feature - 🔖 **Bookmark-Shortcuts** - with number keys --- ## Changes / Fixes - Added native color transfer - Added DaSiWa RTX Upscaler & Refiner

nodes.title: Resolution Note
widgets_values:
# 🛠 Resolution Guide

### 1. Define your "Resolution Preset"
Choose between:
* **Precision Presets:** Optimized Megapixel tiers. **0.52 MP (SD)** is the baseline for most video models.
* **Resolution Presets:** Standard targets (e.g., **1080p**). The node adjusts the actual dimensions to fit your custom aspect ratio while keeping the "1080p density."

### 2. The "Bypass" (No Scale)
* **Toggle ON:** Disables all calculations. The node outputs the exact width/height of the source image/manual input. Use this for native-resolution upscaling or final renders.

| Target MP | Quality | Use case |
|---:|---:|---|
| 0.36 Small | or 480p| ⚠️ Official SVI

nodes.title: 💡 SVI usage / hints
widgets_values:
* Use with 16 or 24 fps (prefer 24)
* Use official resolution 
* It is trained on exact 81 frames, long frame-counts will color shift
* Use different seeds for every video-segment

nodes.title: Resolution and frame count (automated)
widgets_values:
Width & height settings must be divisible by 32 + 1.

Frame count must be 81 for SVI 2.0  (official statement). So it is fixed.

FPS should be 24 (official statement).

a: Total Frames

b: Width

c: Height

d: VRAM Headroom (use 1.0 for 24GB, 2.0 for 16GB, 3.0 for 12 GB, 4.0 for 8 GB)

a: Total Frames

b: Width

c: Height

d: VRAM Headroom (use 1.0 for 24GB, 2.0 for 16GB, 3.0 for 12 GB, 4.0 for 8 GB)
